text.title: Agent Dev Tuning: Kernel Limits and Swap
text.summary: inotify watch limits, PAM nofile/nproc, swap sizing, swappiness, and filesystem noatime tuning for running Claude Code and other AI agents on a Linux VPS.

rule.statement: Set `fs.inotify.max_user_watches = 524288` as the first tuning step on any server running Claude Code. The kernel default (65536 or sometimes 8192) is exhausted on large workspaces, causing "ENOSPC: System limit for number of file watchers reached" errors that look like disk-full.
rule.rationale: Claude Code creates one inotify watch per tracked file. A 50k-file repository with multiple git worktrees active can exceed the default limit immediately. The error message ("ENOSPC") is misleading — disk space is not the issue, the inotify watch table is full.

example.code:
# Apply immediately + persist echo 'fs.inotify.max_user_watches = 524288 fs.inotify.max_user_instances = 1024 fs.inotify.max_queued_events = 65536' | sudo tee /etc/sysctl.d/60-agent-dev.conf sudo sysctl --system
# Verify sysctl fs.inotify.max_user_watches # fs.inotify.max_user_watches = 524288

rule.statement: Set `nofile` to 65535 (soft) and 131072 (hard) for the agent user in `/etc/security/limits.d/`. The default soft limit of 1024 is hit immediately when Claude Code opens many files during workspace indexing.
rule.rationale: Claude Code's workspace indexer, MCP servers, and subprocesses collectively open thousands of file descriptors. The PAM limit gates every process started in the user's session, including systemd user services.

example.code: # /etc/security/limits.d/nero-agent.conf nero soft nofile 65535 nero hard nofile 131072 nero soft nproc 4096 nero hard nproc 8192

rule.statement: Create a 4–8 GB swap file on any server running LLM API calls, even if RAM is abundant. LLM response processing and large context windows cause memory spikes that can trigger OOM kills without swap.
rule.rationale: The Python runtime allocating a 100MB JSON response tree from a large LLM context window is a transient spike. Without swap, the kernel OOM-kills the process. With 4 GB swap and swappiness=10, the spike lands in swap and the process completes normally.

example.code:
sudo fallocate -l 4G /swapfile sudo chmod 600 /swapfile sudo mkswap /swapfile sudo swapon /swapfile echo '/swapfile none swap sw 0 0' | sudo tee -a /etc/fstab sudo findmnt --verify
# Set low swappiness: use RAM first, swap only under pressure echo 'vm.swappiness=10' | sudo tee -a /etc/sysctl.d/60-agent-dev.conf sudo sysctl vm.swappiness=10

rule.statement: Mount the root filesystem with `noatime` to eliminate write operations triggered by file reads. Agent workloads read thousands of source files; each read generates an atime write by default.
rule.rationale: On every file read, the kernel updates the `atime` (access time) field, generating a write. With Claude Code reading 50k files during workspace indexing, this creates 50k unnecessary writes that slow SSD flash cells and compete with real write I/O.

example.code:
# /etc/fstab: add noatime to root mount options # Before: UUID=xxx / ext4 defaults 0 1 # After: UUID=xxx / ext4 defaults,noatime 0 1
# Apply without reboot sudo mount -o remount,noatime /

see-also.link: sysctl drop-in: inotify + memory + file descriptors
tmux session setup, worktree workflow, pre-flight check
